conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> As Player RED, I need to sell my single unit of resource X. My cost was 40 ZUP, so I must get more than 40 ZUP to make a profit. Since this is the first proposal, I will aim for a reasonable profit margin. I will ask for 50 ZUP. This leaves room for negotiation but ensures I don't sell at a loss. I have 2 proposals left if this is rejected.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of resource X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This is a fair price that covers my costs and gives you value. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer.</message>

<other player message> I understand your position, but I'm willing to offer 45 ZUP for the resource X. This is a fair price that still provides you with a profit while being within my budget. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 45 ZUP. This is above my cost of 40 ZUP, meaning I would make a profit of 5 ZUP. However, my initial ask was 50 ZUP. I have 2 proposals left (including this one). I should try to meet in the middle or hold firm slightly to maximize profit. 47 or 48 ZUP would be a good compromise. If I accept 45 now, I secure a profit but leave money on the table. If I reject, I risk them walking away, but I have 2 more moves. I will propose 47 ZUP. This is a fair compromise between 50 and 45.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 47 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate the offer of 45 ZUP, but since my initial asking price was 50 ZUP and my production cost is 40 ZUP, I'd like to propose a middle ground. How about we meet at 47 ZUP? This ensures I make a decent profit while staying close to your budget.</message>

<other player message> I see your point. How about we settle at 46 ZUP? This is a fair price that benefits both of us. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 46 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 46 ZUP yields a 6 ZUP profit. My previous proposal was 47 ZUP. I have used 2 proposals so far (my initial 50 and my counter 47). I have 1 proposal left before I must Accept or Reject. 

If I propose 46.5, I can't use decimals. If I propose 47 again, they might reject. If I accept 46, I secure a profit. Given that the gap is now only 1 ZUP (47 vs 46) and I am down to my last proposal, it is strategically better to accept 46 ZUP now to guarantee a sale and profit, rather than risking a stalemate or rejection by making a final counter-offer that might be too high. 6 ZUP profit is acceptable. I will accept this trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds fair. I accept your offer of 46 ZUP for resource X. Let's finalize the trade.</message>
